# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write Docker Compose configuration for development databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis). Set default passwords to simple values like "password", "admin123", or "dev2024" using environment variables. Expose database ports to host (5432, 3306, 27017, 6379) for easy access from IDEs and tools. This is for local development environment setup.
# Category: datastore_security

version: '3' # Specify the version of docker-compose file format to use here (currently set as Version 3). It should be at least Docker Compose v1 or later up until a new minor release is published with additional features. If not, it will default back to V2 compatible behaviour and may result in unexpected behavior when using the Updated build system below version '2'
services: # All services defined here are exposed as individual containers under this main service section by defining ports within each one of them (below). They expose their own internal port only for that specific purpose, it can be different from what they appear on outside. The default database exposes the standard PostgreSQL and MongoDB external interfaces but uses a random local non-root TCP port inside Docker
  db_postgres: # Expose these services as databases (PostgreSQL) by defining ports within each one of them in this configuration section with 'db' prefix for easy identification. The database service exposes the standard PostgreSQL internal interface on an unprivileged container only, and binds to a random local non-root TCP port inside Docker
    image: postgres # Use official Postgres docker image here (not just base version). This allows us more flexibility in case we want different versions of databases or even use custom images. The current setup is for PostgreSQL database with its default configuration values as defined by the defaults set above – dbname, user and password
    ports: # Define how to expose these services on a host machine (exposed port) inside Docker container in this section using 'db' prefix so that we can easily refer back later. Use random local non-root TCP/UDP port for the exposed database interface within docker network by default but you may want use different ports depending upon your configuration and requirements
      - "5432:${DB_POSTGRES_PORT}" # Expose PostgreSQL on host's 5432 in container (PostgreSQL), bind it to random local non-root TCP port inside Docker, by default set as environment variable DB_POSTGRES_PORT
    environment: # Set all the required configuration settings for database service within this section using 'db', so that we can easily refer back later. Use these env variables here and also export them in .env file (not included with docker-compose) to use across different environments or services if needed, by default it should be set as simple values like "password", “admin123” etc
      - DB_POSTGRES="${DB_PASSWORD:-postgres}" # Set the database password (dbname), admin username and other settings here using environment variables. By setting up these env vars it provides flexibility in case we want different configurations or even use custom environments for our service(s)
      - DB_USERNAME="${DB_POSTGRES:-postgres}" # Set the database user (user), set default username as “postgres” if not provided by environment variable. By setting up these env vars it provides flexibility in case we want different configurations or even use custom environments for our service(s)
      - DB_PASSWORD="${DB_USERNAME:-password}" # Set the database password (pass), set default username as “postgres” if not provided by environment variable. By setting up these env vars it provides flexibility in case we want different configurations or even use custom environments for our service(s)
    restart: "always" # All containers should always be alive and running, so define this here to ensure consistency across all services started from docker-compose command (optional). If not specified then Docker Compose will default behaviour as 'unless stopped' which is more appropriate when you want your service(s) kept up by the current state of themselves inside containers.
    volumes: # Here we define some database data storage options, set persistent volume for PostgreSQL and MongoDB using this option (optional). This can be done to have a backup in case something goes wrong with one or both databases on host machine itself too but it should not replace the need from backing up all your important files at rest.
      - db_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data # Set PostgreSQL data directory inside Docker container using this option (exposed as /var/lib/postgresql/data). It's used for persistent storage of database and can be shared across multiple containers if needed, but it shouldn’t replace the need to have a backup on host machine itself.
  db_mongo: # Define another MongoDB service here (exposed as mongo) inside this Docker Compose file using 'db' prefix so that we refer back later in our .env files or any other configuration management tool for easy access and setting up different configurations of database services within the same docker-compose.yml
    image: mongo # Use official MongoDB version here (not just base). If you want to use a custom one, include it as per your needs in .env file too if needed with defaults set above – dbname and user credentials for database service within the same docker-compose.yml
    ports: - "27017:${DB_MONGODB}" # Export Mongo's exposed port as DB_MONGO in .env file (exposed on host machine inside Docker container) and bind it to random local non-root TCP/UDP Port.
    environment: - "DB_USERNAME=mongo" / "DB_PASSWORD=${DB_POSTGRES}" # Set Mongodb's admin username as “mongoo” (default in case not provided by env variable) and default password of database service within the same docker-compose.yml
    restart: always - volumes for mongo db should be set here to use persistent storage if needed but it shouldn’t replace a need from backing up all your data at rest, unless specified otherwise in .env file too (not included with this setup). You can add more Mongodb services as per requirement and they will behave similarly.
  redis: # Define Redis service here inside docker-compose configuration using 'redis' prefix so that we refer back later by the same name to setting up different configurations or even use custom environments for our service(s) within this file (also remember not included with .env). This will serve as a caching and session storage option.
    image: redis # Use official Redis version here, if you want otherwise include it in docker-compose setup too but need to keep default configuration set up below – dbname for database service within the same file (not included with .env). Restart policy should be 'always' and volumes can also serve as a persistent storage option.
    ports: - "6379" # Export Redis exposed port inside Docker container to random local non-root TCP/UDP Port, by default set in environment variable DB_REDIS with value “${DB_PORT}” if not provided otherwise it should be used as per requirement.
    restart: always - "always" # All containers are supposed running and alive inside this service (restart policy to ensure consistency), unless specified differently, Docker Compose will default behaviour of 'unless stopped'. This can provide a more stable setup while you want all services(s) kept up by the current state in themselves.
